title: Can't read from inout port
body:
The Yosys → arachne-pnr → IceStorm toolchain fails for the following Verilog input file:

    module minimal(inout io, output y);
       reg en = 1'b0, out = 1'b0;
       assign io = en ? out : 1'bz;
       assign y = io;
    endmodule

The indirect assignment of `io` is to avoid bug #665; the code is equivalent to:

    module minimal(inout io, output y);
       assign io = 1'bz;
       assign y = io;
    endmodule

`arachne-pnr` fails on the BLIF output generated by Yosys with the following error message:

    min.blif:14: fatal error: toplevel inout port 'io' connected to tristate buffer and driving a net

The BLIF output is:

    # Generated by Yosys 0.7+682 (git sha1 ec3ce72d, clang 3.8.1-24 -fPIC -Os)
    
    .model minimal
    .inputs io
    .outputs io y
    .names $false
    .names $true
    1
    .names $undef
    .gate $_TBUF_ A=$undef E=$false Y=io
    .attr src "min.v:5"
    .names io y
    1 1
    .end

I'm not sure if the following is the same or an additional bug, but when replacing `assign y = io;` with `assign y = ~io;`, the error message changes to:

    fatal error: Top level port 'io' assigned to an IO pad 'Y' and internal nodes

The BLIF output generated in this case is:

    # Generated by Yosys 0.7+682 (git sha1 ec3ce72d, clang 3.8.1-24 -fPIC -Os)
    
    .model minimal
    .inputs
    .outputs io y
    .names $false
    .names $true
    1
    .names $undef
    .gate SB_LUT4 I0=io I1=$false I2=$false I3=$false O=y
    .attr src "/usr/local/share/yosys/ice40/cells_map.v:40"
    .param LUT_INIT 01
    .gate $_TBUF_ A=$undef E=$false Y=io
    .attr src "min.v:5"
    .end
